calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-22-2007 Failure To Post Jury Fees

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Jun-08-2007 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter came regularly on calendar this day, by assignment from the the Presiding Judge Dept. for Court Trial/Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Commissioner Sue M. Kaplan, Clerk: Sajja Ravinantapricha, CSR: Denise Doucette.
William Fiske, Esq. for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant: Volcano Drywall.
Witenss: Prisciliano Causor, was sworn and direct examined.
No appearance by the Defendant/Cross-Complainant: Eicon, Inc.
Exhibit 1-VD-SGI 581; Exhibit 2-VD-SGI 335; Exhibit 3-VD-SGI 337; Exhibit 4-VD-SGI 336; Exhibit 5-VD-SGI 379; Exhibit 6-VD-SGI 382; Exhibit 7-VD-SGI 401; Exhibit 8-VD-SGI 381; Exhibit 9-VD-SGI 402; Exhibit 10-Invoice #2450-3 dtd 11-04-04 for $12,000.00; Exhibit 11- Invoice #2450-4 for $37,354.00; Exhibit 12- Invoice dtd 04-20-05 for $6,550.49; Exhibit 13- Change Order #3; Exhibit 14-VD-SGI 451 and 452; Exhibit 15-Change Order #4; Exhibit 16-Change Order #5; Exhibit 17-Change Order #6; Exhibit 18-Change Order #7; Exhibit 19-Change Order #8; Exhibit 20-Letter dtd 02-03-05; Exhibit 21-VD-SGI 350 and 405; Exhibit 22-VD-SGI #550,551,548 and 549; Exhibit 23-VD-SGI #132. Exhibits 1 through 23 are ID and EVID.
After hearing the testimony and evidence presented, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff/Cross Defendant and against the Defendant/Cross-Plaintiff.
Judgment granted as to the Complaint as follows: Damages in the amount of $44,695.49. Prejudgment Interest $12,152.88, Costs per memo of costs, Attorney's Fees pursuant to CC. 1717.5.
Judgment on Cross-Complaint is in favor of Cross-Defendant against the Cross-Complainant.
Counsel to prepare Judgment.

June 8, 2007
Judgment rec'd in court and signed by Comm. Kaplan.

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And Production Of Documents And For An Award Of Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-26-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Greg Shaughbessy for Cross-complainant and moving party: Eicon, Inc.
Nandor Krause for Cross-defendant/plaintiff and opposing party: Volcano Drywall

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Cross-complainant Eicon Incorporated's motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents is granted. The objections other than those based on the attorney client and attorney work product privileges are overruled. If any documents are withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege, they shall be identified with particularity in a privilege log. Plaintiff is not required to produce any income tax returns and may redact any social security or taxpayer identification numbers from any documents. Further responses and documents are to be produced within 15 days of notice of this order. The Court finds that Volcano Drywall asserted unmeritorious objections. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $740 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
